license: Public Domain (Sobotta Atlas of Human Anatomy, 1909)
title: Nerves and Vessels of the Arm, Anterior View
description: Classic anatomical drawing detailing the anterior aspect of the arm. It illustrates the pathways of the major nerves (median, ulnar, radial, musculocutaneous) and vessels (brachial artery, basilic and cephalic veins) as they travel from the axilla down toward the cubital fossa, with the biceps brachii and pectoralis major serving as muscular landmarks.
clinical_notes: Supracondylar fractures of the humerus can compress the median nerve and brachial artery in the distal arm. This can lead to median nerve palsy or Volkmann's ischemic contracture. OT interventions include assessing nerve gliding, fabricating static or dynamic orthoses to maintain functional hand positioning, and preventing secondary contractures.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 11 – The Muscular System & Chapter 13 – The Peripheral Nervous System
